rationale: Three corroborated geopolitical indicators meet the escalation threshold. U.S. Section 232 national security investigation into wind turbine imports directly threatens supply chain sourcing for AeroGrid's São Paulo and Santiago service operations. Chinese state-owned enterprise expansion across Argentina, Brazil, and Chile deepens PRC presence in LATAM wind component material flows, creating strategic dependency risk for AeroGrid's live contracts (Enel Chile wind maintenance, Neoenergia turbine service Brazil). Unprecedented regulatory shifts across all three key LATAM markets in 2026 amplify supply chain unpredictability. No cyber indicators confirmed — cyber vector cannot be assessed due to thin collection.
